reforms.description: The UK government has increased tax incentives for research and development, including a temporary business rates relief for small businesses and a 'Patent Box' tax rate of 10% on profits derived from patents. Additionally, a 10% Above the Line (ATL) tax credit for large company R&D investment will increase to 11% from April 2015, alongside a rise in the tax credit for loss-making small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) to 14.5%. The Funding for Lending Scheme was launched to incentivize banks to maintain or expand their lending to households and firms by linking the cost of funding to their lending performance.
reforms.package_name: R&D Tax Incentives and Lending Scheme
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms are designed to enhance R&D investment and improve lending conditions, which are expected to stimulate economic growth and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted and are currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant changes in tax incentives and funding mechanisms aimed at boosting R&D and lending.
reforms.source_quote: In April 2014, the government raised the rate of the tax credit payable to loss-making small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) to 14.5%. From April 2015, the deduction for SME research and development will increase from 225% to 230% of taxable profit.

reforms.description: The government has set up a competitive GBP340 million fund to co-design and co-invest with employers in new skills provision needed to grow their business.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The fund aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, which is essential for business growth and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the fund represents a significant investment in skills development, although it is not as transformative as major reforms.
reforms.source_quote: The government has set up a competitive GBP340 million fund to co-design and co-invest along with employers in new skills provision needed to grow their business.

reforms.description: The National Infrastructure Plan (NIP), launched in 2010, aims to provide a broad vision of the infrastructure investment required to support the UK's long-term growth, identifying major planned and potential investments amounting to GBP460 billion.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The plan is intended to enhance the UK's infrastructure, which is crucial for supporting economic growth and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been enacted and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The NIP represents a significant and comprehensive approach to infrastructure investment.
reforms.source_quote: The National Infrastructure Plan (NIP), launched in 2010, is the first welcome attempt by a UK government to provide a broad vision of the infrastructure investment required to underpin UK’s long-term growth.

reforms.description: The UK established the Green Investment Bank (GIB) to finance infrastructure projects that promote green growth, focusing on innovative and environmentally-friendly technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The GIB is designed to support green technologies, which can drive innovation and sustainable economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The GIB has been established and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the GIB represents a major reform in financing for green infrastructure.
reforms.source_quote: In 2010, the UK established the Green Investment Bank (GIB) and the authorities should expand its operation to emerging low-carbon technologies.

reforms.description: The British Business Bank was established to enhance financing for SMEs by providing funds and guarantees, aiming to unlock up to GBP10 billion of financing for smaller businesses over five years.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The bank is intended to improve access to finance for SMEs, which is crucial for their growth and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The bank has been established and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the British Business Bank represents a significant reform in the support for SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The British Business Bank should continue to address market failures in access to finance for projects with high economic potential.

reforms.description: The Seed Enterprise Investment Scheme (SEIS) was launched to support startups by offering 50% income tax relief on investments made into qualifying small, early-stage companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The SEIS is designed to encourage investment in startups, which can drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been launched and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The SEIS represents a significant but not transformative reform in supporting startup investment.
reforms.source_quote: SEIS offers 50% income tax relief on investments made into qualifying small, early-stage companies.
